behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the model’s code-generation decision, not just local parsing. A stubbed model would hide signature-selection mistakes, so Integration is too low. Not Workflow because this behavior is about one generation output, not a multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run live generation from natural-language specs with real LLM calls, parse produced signature, and assert exact match to expected parameter names/types/return type across multiple specs.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state handoff across local modules (attempt controller, test result handler, early-exit logic). Deterministic stubs can expose ordering/short-circuit bugs. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: correctness does not depend on model output quality, only control flow.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with stubbed generator and stubbed test executor that fail then pass; assert attempt order is 1..n and loop exits immediately on first pass without extra calls.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a live iterative loop and success emerging over retries, which depends on multiple model decisions in sequence (generate -> test feedback -> refine -> regenerate). A mock misses this adaptive behavior. Rejected Agentic-only because behavior text requires sequenced iteration, not one isolated model call.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real end-to-end loop on a known prompt where first attempt commonly fails; run full retries with live model and real feedback injection, and assert pass occurs within <=3 attempts.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module boundary: test runner output must be handed to prompt builder correctly. Deterministic fixtures catch missing/incorrect propagation. Rejected Agentic: this behavior checks prompt content construction, not whether model judgment is good. Rejected System: no explicit real OS/network/subprocess requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Feed realistic failed-test result objects through orchestration into prompt-builder, then assert next prompt contains each failed test name and corresponding failure message.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Requires at least two linked live model steps (initial attempt plus context-aware retry) and validates model-driven adaptation across handoff. Stubs can force different text and hide true failure to use feedback. Rejected Integration/Agentic-only because behavior text is explicitly about cross-attempt response to prior context.
behaviors.test_description: Run live attempt 1, capture failing feedback, run live attempt 2 with refinement context, and assert attempt-2 implementation materially differs and addresses failure-related regions instead of near-identical resubmission.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic retry-limit and bookkeeping behavior across orchestrator + result aggregation modules. Stubs that always fail are sufficient to reveal off-by-one and payload-shape bugs. Rejected System: no required real infra in behavior text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: model output quality is not under test.
behaviors.test_description: Stub generator/test executor to fail every attempt; run loop and assert exactly 3 attempts executed and returned error object includes all three attempt payloads with test results.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly says non-agentic with deterministic stubs; target is local wiring across modules. Rejected Agentic/Workflow by explicit text 'non-agentic' and 'stubbed model outputs'. Rejected System: behavior does not require real OS/network/subprocess; in-process wiring is the stated goal.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end pipeline with deterministic model stub and deterministic test-executor stub over pass/fail scenarios; assert correct call sequencing, state propagation, and attempt bookkeeping.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a multi-step live agent loop with iterative refinement and success criterion after initial failure. Correctness depends on real model behavior across sequential steps, so lower levels miss real failures. Rejected Agentic-only because behavior text requires chained steps (generate -> test -> refine -> retest), not a single model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Use a benchmark function known to fail on first attempt; execute full live pipeline with real model and real retry/refinement flow, then assert first attempt fails and later attempt passes within allowed retries.
